items.title: Passengers from hantavirus ship transferred to Australian quarantine

children.content: A flight carrying five Australians and one New Zealander who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship has landed in an air force base in Perth.

articles.title: No vaccine, no cure: Here's what we know about hantavirus

children.content: As Australian travellers linked to the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak land in Western Australia, we look at what we do and don't know about the virus.

items.title: Coalition tax plan could cost more than suggested in first few years

children.content: The Coalition's plan to index tax brackets could cost $35 billion in the first four years, more than has been suggested, and $44 billion in one year alone in 2035-36.

image.altText: a male politician giving a speech in parliament

items.title: Universities warn against Coalition plan to cut foreign student numbers

children.content: Universities are bracing for foreign student numbers to absorb the bulk of cuts under a Coalition migration plan, with experts warning skilled visas would be difficult to cull.

image.altText: A man in graduation outfit.

items.title: Xi made a historical reference to give Trump a warning. What did it mean?

children.content: The US and China have been rivals for almost 80 years, but when the countries' leaders met in Beijing on Thursday, Xi Jinping made reference to a far more ancient feud.

image.altText: Trump and Xi walk past a group of people standing on steps.

items.title: 'Barbecue stopper' Asian tiger mosquito detected on Australian mainland for the first time

children.content: The world's most invasive mosquito has previously been found at borders, islands, and ports, but has just been discovered in a remote mainland Queensland community.

image.altText: Aedes albopictus female mosquito, also known as the Asian tiger mosquito

items.title: Why millennials and gen Z feel ripped off by this budget

children.content: The prime minister says this budget is about fixing intergenerational inequality, but online, gen Z and the millennials are hearing a different narrative.

articles.title: The budget reply is a reminder times have changed and attention is short

children.content: To appreciate how much times have changed, it is worth comparing the opposition leader's budget reply speech to Peter Dutton's.

articles.title: Angus Taylor promises permanent tax cuts and to reinstate all negative gearing

children.content: The Coalition would repeal all of Labor's negative gearing and capital gains changes and spend $22.5 billion over four years handing back tax "bracket creep" to workers if elected.

image.altText: a male politician giving a speech in a chamber

articles.title: AI to power medicines approvals but humans will still call the shots

children.content: This week’s budget revealed a number of ways the federal government is expected to use artificial intelligence to cut back on red tape. One expert says the benefits are "mostly good".

items.title: How likely is Delta Goodrem to bring home the Eurovision crown?

items.title: Neo-Nazi group banned in Australia under hate laws

children.content: White Australia will be listed as a hate group, making it illegal to support the organisation in any way.

items.title: How Palantir Helps Agencies 'Connect the Dots'

children.content: Palantir doesn’t "spy"—it connects siloed data so agencies can spot patterns fast.
From the CIA/FBI to major companies, the power is in the unified view.
The big question: can we trust it to be used responsibly?

articles.title: Activist, 78, arrested at SA golf course, as protesters chain themselves to machinery

items.title: Ruling upheld after transgender woman excluded from female-only app

children.content: ​A landmark Federal Court ruling that the exclusion of a transgender woman from a female-only app constituted unlawful gender identity discrimination is upheld on appeal.

items.title: Police to hold children up to 48 hours under NT youth justice changes

children.content: Northern Territory Police will be able to hold children in watch houses for up to 48 hours and question them without an adult present under planned changes to the Youth Justice Act.

items.title: Union reaches in-principle deal giving Victorian teachers up to 32pc pay rise

children.content: A drawn out pay dispute between Victoria's government and public sector teachers is set to come to an end, with the union reaching an in-principle deal for pay rises between 28 and 32 per cent.

items.title: Clean-up deadline rejected for contaminated abandoned base
